problem: The page had a paragraph saying 'Demo starts here' instead of a functional skip link, so keyboard and screen reader users could not bypass repeated header and navigation content.
fix_applied: Replaced it with a visible-on-focus skip link pointing to #main and added tabindex='-1' to the main element so the skip target can receive focus.

problem: The provided markup did not define the page language.
fix_applied: Added a full document structure with <html lang='en'> so assistive technologies can use the correct pronunciation and language rules.

problem: The page had no accessible document title.
fix_applied: Added <title>Citylights News</title> to identify the page purpose in browser tabs, bookmarks, and screen reader context.

problem: Tables were used for visual layout, causing assistive technologies to announce meaningless table structures and making the reading order harder to understand.
fix_applied: Replaced layout tables with semantic structural elements including header, nav, main, article, section, aside, figure, and footer, with CSS used for layout.

element: invalid and obsolete HTML including font, acronym, align, bgcolor, background, border, hspace, language, malformed image path, and nested paragraphs
problem: The markup contained obsolete, presentational, and invalid constructs that can interfere with consistent accessibility tree generation.
fix_applied: Rewrote the markup using valid HTML5, CSS for presentation, <abbr> instead of <acronym>, valid image attributes, and valid heading/paragraph structure.

element: decorative border, spacer, marker, and separator images
problem: Many decorative images had missing alt text, which would expose file names or irrelevant image information to screen reader users.
fix_applied: Removed unnecessary decorative image markup from the layout and used CSS borders/backgrounds instead.

problem: The logo alternative text was excessively verbose for an image used as a home link, making navigation inefficient.
fix_applied: Changed the linked logo to have concise alt text, 'Citylights: your access to the city', and added aria-label='Citylights home' to the link to clearly describe the link destination.

problem: The weather graphic had no alternative text.
fix_applied: Marked the image decorative with alt='' and aria-hidden='true' because the weather information is provided as nearby text.

problem: The navigation links used images without alt text, leaving links without accessible names.
fix_applied: Replaced image-based navigation with text links inside a nav landmark and list, giving each link a clear accessible name.

element: navigation links with href='javascript:...' and onfocus='blur();'
problem: The links removed focus and depended on JavaScript URLs, making keyboard navigation unreliable and hiding the user’s current focus position.
fix_applied: Replaced JavaScript links with standard href links and removed focus-blurring behavior.

problem: The original code explicitly blurred focused navigation links and did not provide a reliable visible focus indicator.
fix_applied: Added CSS focus styles for links, buttons, and select controls using a high-visibility outline.

problem: Changing the quick menu selection immediately changed location, creating an unexpected context change without user confirmation.
fix_applied: Converted the quick menu to a labelled form with a separate Go button so the user controls when navigation/submission happens.

problem: The select control had no programmatically associated label.
fix_applied: Added <label for='quickmenu'>Quick menu</label> and an id on the select.

element: headline text using white font color
problem: Several headlines were white text on a white page background, making them unreadable for many users.
fix_applied: Removed the white font styling and applied dark text colors with sufficient contrast.

problem: Headings were visually styled paragraphs instead of semantic heading elements, so the document outline was not available to assistive technology.
fix_applied: Converted the page title to h1, story titles to h2, and subsection/callout titles to h3.

problem: Content sections and callouts were not programmatically labelled.
fix_applied: Added aria-labelledby relationships from articles, sections, and aside content to their visible headings.

problem: The original floated layout and cleared containers could create a confusing reading order compared with the visual order.
fix_applied: Rebuilt the news content as semantic articles in a CSS grid so source order and reading order remain logical.

problem: The chart image had an empty alt attribute even though it conveyed information about brain donations.
fix_applied: Added descriptive alternative text and a visible figure caption identifying the chart and its relationship to the article.

problem: Callouts were implemented as layout tables, which obscured their role as figures and related captions.
fix_applied: Replaced the callout tables with semantic <figure> and <figcaption> elements.

problem: Decorative headline icons had missing alternative text.
fix_applied: Removed the decorative icons from the content and represented headings with text.

problem: The portrait was wrapped in a link without clear link purpose beyond the image alternative text.
fix_applied: Removed the unnecessary link wrapper and kept the image as part of the quoted content with concise alt text.

problem: The attribution for the quotation was plain bold text outside a semantic relationship.
fix_applied: Placed the attribution inside the blockquote footer so it is programmatically associated with the quote.

problem: The original script used document.write and document.all, producing fragile content and invalid markup.
fix_applied: Replaced it with progressive JavaScript that updates a valid <time> element while preserving fallback text.

problem: Traffic and weather information were embedded in layout table cells without a meaningful region or structure.
fix_applied: Grouped the information in a labelled status bar with clear paragraphs and strong labels.

problem: The original page used fixed-width tables that could require horizontal scrolling on small screens or high zoom.
fix_applied: Changed the layout to responsive CSS with max-width, flexible wrapping, and grid columns that reflow.

element: font elements and fixed pixel sizing
problem: Deprecated font elements and rigid layout sizing could interfere with readable text resizing.
fix_applied: Removed font elements and used scalable CSS font sizing and responsive layout.

problem: The current page was not programmatically identified in the navigation.
fix_applied: Added aria-current='page' to the News navigation link.

element: line breaks and empty paragraphs used for spacing
problem:
Empty paragraphs and repeated
elements were used for visual spacing rather than structure.
fix_applied: Removed spacing-only markup and used CSS margins and layout spacing.
